use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::runtime::Runtime;

struct ApiClient {
    runtime: Arc<Runtime>,
    client: reqwest::Client,
}

impl ApiClient {
    fn new() -> Result<Self, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        let runtime = Arc::new(Runtime::new()?);
        let client = reqwest::Client::new();
        Ok(Self { runtime, client })
    }

    fn fetch_user(&self, id: u64) -> Result<User, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        self.runtime.block_on(async {
            let resp = self.client
                .get(&format!("https://api.example.com/users/{}", id))
                .send()
                .await?;
            let user: User = resp.json().await?;
            Ok(user)
        })
    }

    fn process_batch(&self, urls: &[String]) -> Vec<String> {
        self.runtime.block_on(async {
            let mut results = Vec::new();
            for url in urls {
                let resp = self.client.get(url).send().await.unwrap();
                let body = resp.text().await.unwrap();
                results.push(body);
            }
            results
        })
    }
}

struct User { name: String }

fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let client = ApiClient::new()?;
    let _user = client.fetch_user(1)?;
    Ok(())
}
